channel.title: Bimonthly Billing on Utility Explained
channel.description: Recent content in Bimonthly Billing on Utility Explained

item.title: Utility Billing Cycle Explained: How Often Bills Come and What the Dates Mean
item.description: Your electric bill says “Service Period: April 18 – May 17.” Your water bill covers “March 1 – March 31.” Your gas bill shows “February 22 – April 21.” None of them line up with each other or with the calendar month, and trying to track them feels like solving a puzzle. Utility billing cycles are not arbitrary — they are structured around operational logistics, meter-reading schedules, and regulatory requirements. Here is how they actually work.
